rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not invent new declarations.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the model.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Booleans must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings to the correct implementation annex scope; avoid placing implementation-level references in component type contracts.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers are resolved within the visible scope of the component.
Validate that AGREE expressions match the expected data types of referenced ports or variables.
Avoid introducing new data types or package references that are not part of the original AADL model.
Use only declared helper functions or constants that are available in the same annex or imported packages.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Helper functions and constants must be declared before use in the same annex.
Property associations in AADL are not automatically available as AGREE variables.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing references by adding new declarations or architecture elements.
Do not mix implementation-level and type-level references in incorrect annex scopes.
Do not assume AADL property names are directly usable as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
Do not generate expressions with mismatched types (e.g., Boolean expressions for integer variables).
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about stack size properties
AGREE validator diagnostics about property associations
Model fusion or component type annex placement
